title: siler montanum subsp. montanum
intro: Siler montanum subsp. montanum, also known as Mountain savory or Alpine savory, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Siler montanum subsp. montanum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to extract its medicinal compounds.
